what_it_does: Defines an inline SVG icon showing two vertical sort arrows, one pointing upward on the left and one pointing downward on the right.
observations: Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor.
Uses stroke width 1.5 with round caps and joins.
Contains two path elements: a left upward arrow and a right downward arrow.
standouts: The icon is color-inheriting through currentColor.
The two arrows are mirrored in direction and separated horizontally.
The geometry is compact and path-only.
conventions: Follows common outline icon conventions with no fill, rounded strokes, and a 24px coordinate system.
Uses inline path data rather than reusable symbols or groups.
Keeps styling attributes on the root svg element.
notes: A small outline SVG asset for a vertical sort control or sortable column affordance.
questions: Should this icon represent bidirectional sorting, unsorted state, or vertical ordering from this file alone?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a vertical sort icon, and the SVG content depicts vertical opposing sort arrows.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable UI icon for indicating vertical sorting or sortable ordering without embedding a fixed color.
contribution: Contributes a compact, theme-inheriting vertical sort icon asset with bidirectional arrow geometry for use in a fused design system.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24px outline SVG sort-vertical icon made from two stroked paths showing opposite vertical arrows.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a vertical sorting or ordering control in a color-inheriting icon set.
